case_name: Texas Health Harris Methodist Hospital Southwest Fort Worth v. John Davis, Individually and as Guardian of the Person and Estate of Angela Davis, an Incapacitated Person

summary.brief: Texas Health Harris Methodist Hospital Southwest Fort Worth v. John Davis, Individually and as Guardian of the Person and Estate of Angela Davis, an Incapacitated Person. Medical malpractice case involving paediatrics.

evidence_index.text: In May 2014, Angela underwent anesthesia for elective hip surgery at Harris. After receiving a spinal injection, Angela exhibited seizure-like activity, and it was ultimately determined that she had sustained very significant brain damage.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: medication tranexamic acid was accidentally injected into her spine instead of the anesthetic agent bupivacaine.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: At the time of Angela’s injury, Sundance Anesthesia, P.A. had an exclusive contract with Harris to serve as the hospital’s anesthesia provider.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Goforth focused on setting up the anesthesia machine with the equipment that she would need during the procedure, Tucker drew up the medications.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Tucker removed the anesthesia-related medications from the drawer of the Pyxis machine, affixed preprinted labels to the syringes identifying the medications to be drawn, and then proceeded to fill the syringes with the necessary medications.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Following the spinal injection, Angela began kicking her legs and displaying other seizure-like activity.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Davis, acting both in his individual capacity and as guardian of Angela’s estate, filed suit against Harris, Sundance, Dr. Leitch, Goforth, and Tucker.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: we reverse the trial court’s judgment and remand this case for a new trial.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Angela was scheduled for elective hip surgery and was to receive spinal anesthesia.

focused_exam.system: Pre-operative assessment not detailed in judgment

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Preparation for elective hip surgery with spinal anesthesia

timeline_phases.name: Pre-Operative Assessment
timeline_phases.description: Patient assessed and prepared for elective hip surgery

timeline_phases.name: Surgical Procedure
timeline_phases.description: Administration of spinal anesthesia and subsequent surgery

decision_points.clinical_context: Preparation and administration of spinal anesthesia
decision_points.prompt: What verification process should be followed before administering spinal anesthesia?

options.description: Implement a dual-verification process for medication labeling
options.clinical_reasoning: Standard practice to prevent medication errors

options.description: Proceed with single verification by CRNA

actual_action_defendant.description: Single verification by CRNA was used

expected_action_court.description: A dual-verification process should have been implemented
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony and Joint Commission standards

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to implement dual-verification led to medication error
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Dual-verification would have prevented the error
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient experienced seizure-like activity following incorrect medication administration.

key_findings: Trial court's judgment reversed and remanded for a new trial.

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Correct anesthetic agent would have prevented adverse effects

factual_timeline.event: Angela underwent anesthesia for elective hip surgery
Patient scheduled for elective hip surgery
Angela underwent anesthesia for elective hip surgery

allegations.allegation: Failure to implement a medication-handling policy

court_findings.legal_significance: Breach led to severe brain damage

court_findings.finding: Failure to implement a medication-handling policy caused injury
court_findings.legal_significance: Breach of duty and causation established

court_findings.finding: Trial court's judgment reversed and remanded for a new trial
